best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest contributor: 31 trades, $2413.2 PnL, 192.96 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. It also owns the current -638.75 MAE, so expansion should not be blunt.
charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 7 trades but $1060.6 PnL, 224.54 PnL/exposure hour, 12-point median TP, no slow trades, and best profile-level worst MAE among major active profiles at -346.8.

breakout_long_1dte produced 20 trades, $1298.7 PnL, 167.94 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow trades, and worst MAE -375.7. Prior evidence says direct 1DTE tightening hurts; companion split is better than replacing this edge.

charm_pin_pullback_long produced 31 trades, $1160.2 PnL, median hold 2.97 minutes, avg MAE -71.39, and 178.07 PnL/exposure hour. Its -518.5 worst MAE argues for narrow tail isolation, not broad profile pruning.

best_edges.edge: Expired/stale no-0DTE fallback has positive but thin evidence
best_edges.evidence: No-0DTE fallback total is $369.2 on 6 trades. EXPIRED_0DTE breakout_long_no_0dte is very efficient but single-trade only; STALE_0DTE pullback is cleaner than stale breakout by MAE and exposure efficiency.

broken_edges.evidence: pullback_long contributes most PnL but also the global worst MAE -638.75, 187.9 minute hold, no early positive progress, weak ES structure score 3, negative higher-low/higher-high distances, and only $35 PnL on the tail.
broken_edges.problem: Largest edge is also the deepest hidden-MAE source; global admission loosening would likely increase capital tie-up.

broken_edges.evidence: The extended-VWAP rejection near-miss cluster covers all 24 known bad-MAE rows, but protected extended-VWAP winners also exist: 17 trades, $632.4 PnL, median hold 1.18 minutes, median MAE -15.3. Broad VWAP caps would kill real edge.
broken_edges.problem: Bad and good trades share extension features; only surgical veto-boundary research is justified.

broken_edges.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long is enabled and routed but produced 0 trades in run_048. Earlier runs showed 2 high-PnL trades but also -655.2 MAE, and recent sample-builder changes were flat at best.
broken_edges.problem: Unreliable reachability and two-trade historical tail risk; do not spend the main profit-expansion budget here unless isolated as a sample-builder holdout.

broken_edges.evidence: squeeze_long is enabled and routed but produced 0 trades in run_048. Earlier squeeze TP expansion was accepted historically, but latest evidence gives no live attribution.
broken_edges.problem: No current sample; avoid tuning exits or gates until reachability is explained.

broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long have zero current trades or are disabled. Historical optimizer leaders do not require them.
broken_edges.problem: Dormant or inactive active-router profiles with no current edge attribution.

market_regime_read: Current regime is a short 12.3-day, high-win-rate SPX500 long scalper with take-profit-only exits. There are no realized losses and no >=12h slow trades, but path stats show hidden risk: average MAE -106.25, worst MAE -638.75, only 10.5% of trades had MFE before MAE, and TP before MAE happened only 1.1% of the time. The leader family around run_044 has higher net profit and score than run_048, while robust leaderboard variants around run_029 have cleaner drawdown and worst MAE. Profit expansion should therefore prioritize convex profile/sample expansion that preserves no-slow behavior, while treating hidden MAE as the gating constraint.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate 1DTE pullback/reclaim behavior from breakout continuation without disturbing breakout_long_1dte.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Companion profile may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or be unreachable under current router ordering.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY entries with ES pullback/reclaim features, profile attribution, MAE/MFE path stats, and breakout_long_1dte cannibalization report.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state looks like pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1298.7, 1DTE average/worst MAE no worse than baseline, total net profit >=98% of leader, and total score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Detect delayed-recovery heat before capital is tied up for long periods.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Path-aware logic can overfit delayed green recoveries and cut protected fast winners unless validated by protected UTC retention.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Early-Progress Heat Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade first-profit timing, first nonzero move, MFE-before-MAE, half-TP touch time, protected fast-winner UTCs, and profile-specific bad-MAE rows.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: pullback_long or charm_pin_pullback_long entries that fail to make meaningful early positive progress before adverse expansion.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce worst MAE toward <= -513 and preferably <= -403, retain >=90% combined pullback/charm-pin PnL, retain >=95% protected fast winners, keep slow_12h at 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Use existing dormant playbook profile to separate charm-wall/pin-avoid regimes.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant activation may be unreachable or may steal high-quality charm_pin/charm_wall trades without improving portfolio score.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Attribution Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution, profile standalone PnL, combined charm-pin/charm-wall PnL, protected fast-winner retention, and worst-MAE attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm/pin context near wall where pin-avoid logic may isolate tail-heavy pullbacks.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile has positive standalone PnL or lowers combined charm/pin worst MAE, total net profit >=98% of leader, protected fast-winner retention >=95%.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Revive call-wall acceptance only as an isolated sample-builder, not a portfolio-wide expansion.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Looser call-wall gates can admit chop or reproduce the historical -655.2 MAE tail while adding few trades.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Isolated Sample Builder
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: call_wall_acceptance_long standalone trades, acceptance duration, forward DEX, net DEX, call-volume expansion, ES-VWAP extension, MAE path.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance with positive forward DEX, call-volume expansion, and sustained strike acceptance.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 3 call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive standalone PnL, PnL/exposure >=150 initially, worst MAE <= -200, and total net profit >=98% of leader.

next_experiments.config_bias: Preserve wide/convex TP behavior and require-breakout. Explore only narrow admission/sample-builder variants around wall support, higher-high/higher-low confirmation, and bounded distance above charm wall. Do not compress the 12-point charm-wall target unless exposure efficiency improves without reducing profile PnL.
next_experiments.goal: Increase high-convexity charm-wall contribution without worsening hidden MAE.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The best profit-expansion candidate is not more pullback flow, but more tightly supported charm-wall continuation flow, because current charm_wall_breakaway_long has high PnL/hour and cleaner worst MAE on a small sample.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Convex Admission Follow-Through
next_experiments.primary_metric: Total risk-adjusted score, charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL/exposure >=225, total net profit >=98% of run_044, global worst MAE no worse than leader-family tolerance, slow_12h = 0.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample can overstate expectancy; loosening wall geometry can admit weak continuation attempts or cannibalize pullback/charm-pin winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use the enabled extended-VWAP rejection near-miss family as the research path. Bias toward tiny boundary nudges that catch the specific bad-MAE rows while explicitly protecting fast and extended-VWAP winner UTCs. Avoid broad ES-VWAP max caps, setup floors, structure-score floors, or global RSI/ADX floors.
next_experiments.goal: Improve risk-adjusted profit by removing long low-PnL heat without killing the fast extended-VWAP edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The bad-MAE cluster is fully covered by extended-VWAP near misses, but protected winners share the same regime; a surgical boundary study has better expectancy than broad pruning.
next_experiments.name: Protected-Winner Extended-VWAP Tail Boundary Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE improvement, retained total PnL >=98% of leader, protected fast-winner retention >=95%, protected extended-VWAP winner retention >=95%, PnL/exposure flat or better.
next_experiments.risk: The worst bad-MAE trades still closed green; too much veto expansion can reduce net profit more than it improves hidden MAE.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte baseline thresholds stable. If legal, route a companion 1DTE pullback split as an attribution experiment rather than tightening the existing breakout profile. Measure cannibalization explicitly.
next_experiments.goal: Extract additional 1DTE edge or reduce 1DTE MAE without damaging the proven breakout fallback.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY is material and stable enough to protect; direct tightening has historically hurt, so profile split research is more promising than parameter tightening.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Router Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, breakout_long_1dte retained trades/PnL, total score, total net profit >=98% of leader.
next_experiments.risk: Companion routing may be unreachable or may steal profitable breakout trades without adding net PnL.

next_experiments.config_bias: Run exactly one dormant valid-0DTE activation as an attribution probe if the optimizer supports full router-list replacement. Prefer charm_wall_pin_avoid_long or late_day_pin_avoid_long over unrelated inactive concepts. Keep same-profile tuning minimal.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether an existing dormant pin-avoid profile can separate tail-prone charm/pullback states from clean convex winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Tail-heavy charm/pullback rows and clean charm-wall rows may be different regimes already represented by dormant playbook profiles; attribution should precede further broad tuning.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Attribution Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Activated profile standalone PnL, combined charm-pin/charm-wall worst MAE, total net profit retention, protected fast-winner retention, total risk-adjusted score.
next_experiments.risk: Router activation can cannibalize strong existing profiles or produce no trades; reject unless attribution is clearly positive or MAE improves with high PnL retention.

next_experiments.config_bias: Isolate STALE_0DTE and EXPIRED_0DTE behavior if possible. Do not disturb VALID_0DTE routing. Favor pullback_no_0dte over stale breakout if forced to choose, because stale breakout has lower PnL/hour and worse average MAE.
next_experiments.goal: Improve fallback efficiency while preserving the small positive fallback contribution.
next_experiments.hypothesis: No-0DTE fallback is positive but uneven: EXPIRED_0DTE is a one-trade winner, stale pullback is cleaner, and stale breakout ties up more exposure for less return.
next_experiments.name: No-0DTE Data-State Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL/trade count, total net profit >=98% of leader, slow_12h = 0.
next_experiments.risk: Sample is only 6 trades; state-specific changes can remove the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner or overfit stale behavior.

next_experiments.config_bias: Only attempt an isolated call_wall_acceptance_long sample builder if it does not loosen global option-flow behavior. Treat it as lower priority than charm-wall and tail-boundary work.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether call_wall_acceptance_long is unreachable, over-filtered, or simply absent in this sample.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The profile is enabled and routed but has zero trades in run_048; earlier high-PnL/tail evidence makes it a discovery branch, not a core profit-expansion path.
next_experiments.name: Call Wall Reachability Attribution Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: call_wall_acceptance_long trade count, standalone PnL, PnL/exposure, worst MAE, total PnL retention, and cannibalization of pullback/charm-wall trades.
next_experiments.risk: Historical call-wall tails were severe; adding trades without path containment can lower risk-adjusted score.

research_notes: Do not chase raw win rate; all completed trades are winners, so the optimizer should rank by score, net PnL, PnL/exposure, protected-winner retention, and hidden MAE. Current run_048 is below the run_044 leader and robust run_029 family, so profit expansion should be incremental and reversible. Avoid upstream timing changes such as EMA/ADX period changes because experiment memory shows severe degradation. Avoid broad setup-quality floors and global structure/VWAP caps because protected winner fingerprints overlap with bad-MAE states.
risk_controls: Reject any experiment that creates slow_12h trades or materially increases exposure hours without proportional PnL.
Require total net profit >=98% of run_044 for expansion experiments unless MAE improvement is large enough to justify a risk-reducer branch.
Require protected fast-winner retention >=95% for any entry-veto or admission-tightening study.
Track profile cannibalization explicitly for 1DTE companion, dormant pin-avoid, and call-wall sample-builder experiments.
Treat any broad filter that blocks extended-VWAP winners as suspect unless worst MAE and score improve materially.
Do not optimize inactive zero-trade profiles unless the experiment is explicitly a router attribution probe.
